items.title: After 14 years in visa limbo, Ferdos felt conflicted as Iran’s soccer team gained asylum
items.summary: The 23-year-old said it was hard to understand how the government was able to act so swiftly, when "for us, it's been years and decades".

items.title: 'I literally have no choice': The workers bearing the brunt of Australia's fuel crisis
items.summary: In response to fuel shortages, some governments recommend people work from home. But for those who can't, the bill is rising.

items.title: 'No-one is feeling it more': New measures to protect truckies from fuel price spikes
items.summary: Under the amendment, truck drivers and road support businesses will be able to make an emergency application for a contract chain order.

items.title: This fuel costs as little as 80c a litre in Australia. So why isn't everyone using it?
items.summary: This fuel's slow demise came with the introduction of electric vehicles and the death of the local car manufacturing industry.

items.title: Iran denies Trump's claim of negotiations after US delays strikes on power plants
items.summary: Donald Trump says there have been "very strong talks" between the US and Iran, but an Iranian official has described it as "fake news".

items.title: Death toll rises to 66 after Colombian air force plane crash
items.summary: Authorities are still investigating the cause of the crash.

items.title: Fresh death cap mushroom warning for foragers as weather could lead to fungi flourishing
items.summary: As mushrooms spring up across the country, Australians are being urged to only buy mushrooms from reputable markets and grocers.

items.title: Amid new guidelines, a warning data centres could 'cut corners', threaten energy, water supply
items.summary: Australia has the second-largest pipeline of data centre construction in the world, after the US.

items.title: UK PM says ambulances targeted in 'deeply shocking antisemitic arson attack'
items.summary: Four ambulances belonging to a Jewish organisation were set afire in what police are treating as an antisemitic hate crime.

items.title: Two dead, dozens injured after Air Canada jet collides with fire truck at New York airport
items.summary: The pilot and co-pilot were killed after the flight landed at New York's LaGuardia airport from Montreal.

items.title: Central Land Council push increased support for remote communities as fuel and living costs skyrocket
items.summary: The last increase to the remote rental allowance occurred more than 25 years ago. Dr Josie Douglas says the payment has failed to keep up the reality of the cost of living for many in remote areas.

items.title: Energy watchdog chief warns Australia, says oil shock worse than past crises combined
items.summary: Asia is at the forefront of the energy crisis, the head of the International Energy Agency has told an Australian audience.

items.title: Government says people can 'make the call' on work from home amid fuel supply concerns
items.summary: The world's top energy agency has called for Australian businesses to allow employees to work from home amid concerns over fuel supply.

items.title: Relief for drivers as rideshare giants raise prices amid fuel supply concerns
items.summary: Uber and DiDi are among the businesses increasing their fees in response to the fuel supply issues triggered by the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Thinking of buying an electric vehicle? Here's what to know before making the switch
items.summary: Soaring petrol prices are prompting some Australians to consider buying an electric vehicle.

items.title: Far from the frontline, close to the fallout: How the Middle East war is testing Australia
items.summary: Australia is far from the Middle East conflict — but not untouched by it. As risks ripple outwards, Canberra's role is quietly being tested.

items.title: 'No viable alternative': Gove community hits back at Woolworths after closure announcement
items.summary: Rirratjingu Aboriginal Corporation is calling on Woolworths to renegotiate with community about the closure which would leave Nhulunbuy, the fourth largest town in the Northern Territory, at risk of severe food insecurity.

items.title: Kyro wanted gender-affirming treatment but was told he had to wait to make the decision
items.summary: Most young people in Australia need the consent of a guardian to access gender-affirming care. In some parts of the country, there are additional restrictions.

items.title: 'A party of disaffection': Does One Nation's historic surge in SA signal a broader trend?
items.summary: One Nation's rise reflects voter frustration and isn't necessarily a long-term shift, one political expert has told SBS News.

items.title: Israel destroys infrastructure in Lebanon, raising concerns about 'immense' civilian harm
items.summary: The destruction of bridges and homes on Sunday marks a significant escalation in Israel's military campaign in Lebanon.

items.title: 'There is a risk': Concerns over the new Iranian leader owning assets in Australia
items.summary: Multiple reports show Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, owns a multi-million-dollar portfolio of luxury assets worldwide.

items.title: Iran pledges to target vital Middle East infrastructure after Trump's Strait of Hormuz threat
items.summary: Iran's military has said US energy and desalination facilities in the region would be targeted if Iranian power plants were struck.

items.title: Rhoda Roberts AO: A defining voice of her generation
items.summary: Rhoda Roberts AO carried her culture and identity with unapologetic confidence and staunch purpose.

items.title: Becoming a 'slay queen' gave Habiba a life of luxury, but her lifestyle can come with a cost
items.summary: In South Africa, women who profit from relationships with rich men are known as 'slay queens'. But there can be devastating consequences.

items.title: Australia to face price 'bumps' but not fuel shortage despite oil tanker shipment cancellations
items.summary: Six tankers scheduled to deliver oil to Australia have had their voyages cancelled as impacts from the war in the Middle East begin to bite.
